---
title: "LRPLF01: Level factors for word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPLF01 is a **level-factors** model for word reading (`ewrswr`): what is associated
with the word-reading score *level* at each of the four timepoints (t1–t4),
modelled on the logit scale with a Beta-Binomial likelihood and a child random
intercept. Unlike the gain model it is **not autoregressive** — there is no own
baseline; per-timepoint intercepts absorb the maturation trend.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling + the level-model crossover caveat"}

Under the locked study DAG every non-randomised coefficient is an **adjusted
association** confounded by latent general ability (the child random intercept
repairs the time-invariant part) — "associated with", never "drives". **Crucially,
the group effect is modelled as `group × time` (a per-timepoint group coefficient
`b_grp_time[t]`, read as trajectory divergence) because after t2 the waitlist
crosses over: the group difference across the four timepoints is *not* a clean ITT
contrast.** The clean randomised contrast lives **only at t2** (`b_grp_time[1]`,
labelled *causal* in the table below); the t3/t4 group coefficients are
post-crossover and are cohort/timing associations, not treatment effects. SES is
excluded (non-DAG / redundant).

:::

**DAG factor set:** group $\times$ time · age (per timepoint) · cognitive ability
(block design) $\times$ time · group $\times$ ability · child random intercept.

## Model

$$
\operatorname{logit}\mu_{i,t} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{time}[t]}
  + b_{\text{grp}[t]}\,\text{group}_i
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std},t}
  + g_{\text{ability}[t]}\,z(\text{blocks})
  + \gamma_{\text{grp\_ability}}\,\text{group}_i\,z(\text{blocks})
  + u_{\text{child}[i]},
$$

with $W_t \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$, $N = 79$.
The per-timepoint group ($b_{\text{grp}[t]}$) and ability ($g_{\text{ability}[t]}$)
coefficients are the `group × time` and `ability × time` interactions; $t=1$ is t2,
the clean randomised contrast. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
